steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Words in Context question testing precise vocabulary. The skill is picking the word that captures exactly what dense vegetation does to the distance high-frequency sounds can travel. The decisive clue is the causal logic: researchers believe dense-habitat birds sing at lower frequencies because dense vegetation ______ the distance high-frequency sounds travel — so the blank must mean “reduces.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — exceed: “Exceed” means to surpass or go beyond. Saying dense vegetation exceeds the distance sounds travel is incoherent — vegetation cannot be greater than a distance, and the idea is that it shortens that distance, not surpasses it. ✗
  • Choice B — diminish: “Diminish” means to reduce or make smaller. Dense vegetation diminishes the distance high-frequency sounds can travel — this explains why birds in those habitats switch to lower frequencies that carry farther. ✓
  • Choice C — encompass: “Encompass” means to enclose or encircle. A distance is not a physical object that can be enclosed by vegetation; this word does not address what happens to sound propagation. ✗
  • Choice D — conceal: “Conceal” means to hide or keep from being seen. The passage is about how far sounds travel, not whether they are hidden; concealing a distance makes no sense in this context. ✗
That is the correct answer.
